title: Homepage chainsaw background, Schoolhouse sneak peek, new booths (2027/2028), Laser Hell zone, and dedicated pages (hash routing)

requirements.summary: Add a chainsaw-themed AI background image as a subtle site-wide background layer while preserving readability.
acceptanceCriteria: A new chainsaw-themed background image is added as a static asset under frontend/public/assets/generated and is used in the homepage/global background layer.
Foreground content (hero text, cards, navigation) remains readable; background is subtly blended (e.g., via opacity/overlay) rather than overpowering.
No changes are made to any files under frontend/src/components/ui or other immutablePaths.

file_operations.description: Add the required AI-generated chainsaw-themed background static asset at this exact path/filename so it can be referenced by the global background layer.

Add a fixed-position image layer referencing frontend/public/assets/generated/chainsaw-home-bg.dim_2400x1350.jpg with conservative opacity and a dark/gradient overlay to maintain strong text contrast/readability across all sections.

requirements.summary: Add a Schoolhouse Haunted House sneak-peek section on the homepage with an AI-generated scenic elevation image and English description, reachable via an anchor id.
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage includes a clearly titled Schoolhouse sneak-peek section with an AI-generated concept scenic elevation image and an English description.
Section is reachable via an on-page anchor id and fits the existing styling patterns (uses the shared Section + Card layout where appropriate).

file_operations.description: Add the required AI-generated Schoolhouse concept scenic elevation static asset at this exact path/filename.
Create content for the Schoolhouse sneak peek (English title, description text, and any small metadata needed by both the homepage section and the dedicated page).

Implement a new homepage section using the existing Section layout and Card styling patterns, including anchor id (e.g., "schoolhouse-sneak-peek"), rendering the image from frontend/public/assets/generated/schoolhouse-sneak-peek-elevation.dim_1600x900.jpg and the English description from content.
Wire the new SchoolhouseSneakPeekSection into the homepage flow in a sensible position without creating orphan UI (ensure it renders as part of the homepage view).

requirements.summary: Create a dedicated Schoolhouse sneak-peek page with a stable route and link to it from the homepage section.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a dedicated Schoolhouse sneak-peek page with a stable URL/route within the frontend.
Homepage Schoolhouse sneak-peek section includes a working link/button to open the dedicated page.
The dedicated page renders correctly on refresh (direct navigation to the URL works).

file_operations.description: Create a dedicated page view that displays the Schoolhouse sneak peek image (frontend/public/assets/generated/schoolhouse-sneak-peek-elevation.dim_1600x900.jpg) and the same (or expanded) English description from content, with an obvious way to return to the homepage.

Add hash-based routing (e.g., using window.location.hash parsing) to render either the homepage or the new SchoolhouseSneakPeekPage at a stable route (e.g., "#/schoolhouse"). Ensure direct navigation/refresh works because hash routes are client-only.

Add a clear CTA/link (e.g., "View Sneak Peek") that navigates to the dedicated hash route for the Schoolhouse page (e.g., "#/schoolhouse") while preserving the on-page anchor id behavior for the section.

requirements.summary: Add the Slider Doom food booth (Coming 2027) with exact menu text, AI description, homepage card image via generatedImages mapping, and a dedicated page.
acceptanceCriteria: Food booth “Slider Doom” appears in the Food Booths grid on the homepage with label “Coming 2027”, an English description, and menu containing the exact text “Fries. Loaded sparks”.
A new AI-generated image is available for Slider Doom and is wired through the generatedImages mapping so the booth card displays an image.
A dedicated Slider Doom page exists with the booth’s name, label/year, image, description, and menu, and the booth card (or a CTA) links to that page.

file_operations.description: Add the required AI-generated Slider Doom booth key art static asset at this exact path/filename.

Append a new booth entry for “Slider Doom” with label exactly “Coming 2027”, an English horror-tone tagline/description, and menu containing the exact text item “Fries. Loaded sparks” (as a string item or an object name that renders exactly that text).

Wire the Slider Doom image into the generatedImages.foodBooths mapping so the Food Booths card can resolve and display it (map “Slider Doom” to the path for frontend/public/assets/generated/booth-slider-doom.dim_1400x900.jpg).
Create a dedicated Slider Doom page view that renders the booth name, label/year, image, English description, and menu, matching the homepage content.

Add internal linking from the Slider Doom booth card (or a CTA within the card) to the dedicated hash route (e.g., "#/food-booths/slider-doom"). Ensure existing booth cards remain unchanged in behavior except for adding non-breaking link support.
Register the Slider Doom dedicated page in the hash-based routing table so direct navigation/refresh works for the stable route.

requirements.summary: Add the Sharks Hell food booth (Coming 2028) with exact menu items, AI description, homepage card image via generatedImages mapping, and a dedicated page.
acceptanceCriteria: Food booth “Sharks Hell” appears in the Food Booths grid on the homepage with label “Coming 2028”, an English description, and the menu items listed exactly as provided.
A new AI-generated image is available for Sharks Hell and is wired through the generatedImages mapping so the booth card displays an image.
A dedicated Sharks Hell page exists with the booth’s name, label/year, image, description, and menu, and the booth card (or a CTA) links to that page.

file_operations.description: Add the required AI-generated Sharks Hell booth key art static asset at this exact path/filename.

Append a new booth entry for “Sharks Hell” with label exactly “Coming 2028”, an English horror-tone tagline/description, and menu items listed exactly as: “Cookie dough sharks cookie”, “stuffed ice cream”, “chocolate white chocolate”, “Laser beam Shake”.

Wire the Sharks Hell image into generatedImages.foodBooths mapping (map “Sharks Hell” to the path for frontend/public/assets/generated/booth-sharks-hell.dim_1400x900.jpg) so the homepage booth card displays the image.
Create a dedicated Sharks Hell page view that renders the booth name, label/year, image, English description, and exact menu list.
Add internal linking from the Sharks Hell booth card (or a CTA within the card) to the dedicated hash route (e.g., "#/food-booths/sharks-hell").
Register the Sharks Hell dedicated page in the hash-based routing table so direct navigation/refresh works for the stable route.

requirements.summary: Add the Laser Hell scare zone with AI-written English description, AI-chosen location, generated image mapping, homepage display, and a dedicated page.
acceptanceCriteria: “Laser Hell” appears in the homepage Scare Zones grid with an English tagline/description and an AI-generated image wired via the generatedImages mapping.
The location for Laser Hell is clearly displayed (either as a dedicated field rendered in UI or included as a clearly labeled line in the content).
A dedicated Laser Hell page exists and includes the zone name, image, description, and location, with a working navigation link from the homepage zone card (or CTA).

file_operations.description: Add the required AI-generated Laser Hell scare zone key art static asset at this exact path/filename.
Add a new scare zone entry for “Laser Hell” including an English horror-tone tagline/description and an explicit English location field/value (AI-chosen).

Wire the Laser Hell image into generatedImages.scareZones mapping (map “Laser Hell” to the path for frontend/public/assets/generated/zone-laser-hell.dim_1400x900.jpg) so the scare zone card displays an image.
Render the Laser Hell location clearly in the scare zone card UI (e.g., a labeled line), and add a non-breaking link/CTA from the zone card to the dedicated Laser Hell page route.
Create a dedicated Laser Hell page view including the zone name, image, English description, and clearly labeled location, plus a return-to-home link.
Register the Laser Hell dedicated page in the hash-based routing table so direct navigation/refresh works for the stable route.

requirements.summary: Update header navigation and internal linking so new dedicated pages are discoverable without breaking existing smooth-scroll anchor navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Header navigation continues to work for existing homepage anchors (e.g., #auditions, #food-booths, #scare-zones).
Users can reach each new dedicated page from the homepage (via section CTAs/cards) and optionally via header navigation without confusing anchor-only behavior.
No console errors related to routing/navigation in normal browsing.

file_operations.description: Update navigation handling to preserve smooth-scroll for homepage anchors while also supporting navigation when currently on a dedicated hash route (e.g., when clicking an anchor item, first navigate to the homepage hash and then scroll). Optionally add a small “Pages”/“Sneak Peek” link group that uses hash routes for Schoolhouse, Slider Doom, Sharks Hell, and Laser Hell without interfering with anchor-only items.
Ensure booth cards/CTAs link to the dedicated pages for Slider Doom and Sharks Hell while keeping the section anchor id (#food-booths) behavior intact.
Ensure the Laser Hell scare zone card/CTA links to its dedicated page while keeping the section anchor id (#scare-zones) behavior intact.
Ensure the Schoolhouse homepage section includes a clear link/CTA to its dedicated page and that the section remains reachable via its on-page anchor id.
